name: Humane AI Pin
short_description: A screenless, magnetically worn AI device with a camera, mics, and a laser projector for the palm.
long_description: A clothing-clipped wearable that tried to replace the phone screen with voice, a projected palm display, and an always-available assistant. Commercially short-lived (sales wound down in 2025), it remains a sharp reference point for ambient, screen-light body-worn AI.

images.caption: Humane AI Pin and charging case.
images.alt_text: A small white square Humane AI Pin device with a chrome edge and an illuminated multicolor Trust Light, resting on top of its rounded white charging case.

sources.title: Humane AI Pin
sources.access_notes: Official site; product later discontinued — see Open Questions.

interactions.title: Hold two fingers and become an interpreter
interactions.description: Press and hold two fingers on the chest pad, say a sentence, and the Pin speaks it back in the target language; hold again while the other person replies and it translates them into English, turning the device into a hands-free interpreter mid-conversation.
interactions.source_label: Humane support — Use Ai Pin Interpreter

interactions.title: Catch me up
interactions.description: Say "catch me up" and the Pin reads through your missed messages, calls, and reminders and narrates a spoken summary, so you never pull out a phone to triage an inbox.

interactions.title: A green interface painted on your palm
interactions.description: Raise your open hand and the laser ink display projects a green UI onto your palm; tilt and pinch your hand to scroll and select, reading an incoming call or a menu without a screen anywhere in sight.

interactions.title: Hold up your lunch and ask if it fits your goals
interactions.description: Point the camera at a piece of food you're holding and the Pin identifies it and tells you whether it lines up with your fitness goals, using its vision model instead of a barcode or app.

interactions.title: Wait ten seconds for the weather, then get it wrong
interactions.description: Reviewers found everyday asks — the day's weather, a simple fact — could take many seconds to answer and sometimes came back confidently wrong, with MKBHD calling it the worst product he'd reviewed; the gap between the ambient pitch and the lived latency became the story.

interactions.title: The day every Pin became a paperweight
interactions.description: On February 28, 2025 Humane shut down its servers and customer data was deleted; because every core feature ran in the cloud, the $699 devices were left able to do little more than report their own battery level — a body-worn assistant that outlived its mind.

interactions.title: Glance-and-narrate a museum
interactions.description: Look at an artwork or a plaque and ask for a quiet plain-language explanation read into your ear — grounded in the on-board camera and vision-language model the Pin already runs for object and food recognition.

interactions.title: A recording light other people can read
interactions.description: Because the Trust Light visibly lights whenever the mics or camera are live and there is no always-on wake word, a bystander could glance at your chest to know whether they're being captured — a consent signal designed into the hardware rather than buried in settings.
